title: Finite Gaussian Elimination Checks

solver_reuse.target: finite Gaussian elimination bad eliminated-RHS QF_LRA/Farkas regression seed
solver_reuse.pressure: Exact row-operation replay computes the eliminated RHS as 7 while a malformed row claims 8, isolating a compact equality conflict for the Farkas route.
solver_reuse.next_step: Promote additional elimination rows only when they add distinct pivoting, singularity, rank-revealing, or numerical-honesty pressure; keep general algorithm correctness and floating-point stability in Lean or numerical-honesty lanes.

graduation_criteria: Encode the fixed rational matrix, right-hand side, pivot multiplier, row operation, determinant product, and back-substitution result as deterministic exact-rational replay obligations.
Route the false eliminated-RHS row through a source-linked QF_LRA artifact and checked Farkas evidence after exact replay computes the conflicting scalar.
Expose the pack through the matrix computation learner and consumer query surfaces.
Keep general Gaussian-elimination correctness, pivoting strategy, rank-revealing variants, sparse fill-in, conditioning, and floating-point stability marked Lean or numerical-honesty horizon.
